criteria.name: Problem Identification
criteria.description: Ability to identify the core components of the capital budgeting problem, including cash flows, discount rates, and time horizon.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Clearly identifies all relevant components and their interdependencies within the capital budgeting context.
criteria.poor_indicator: Fails to identify key components or misinterprets their relevance to the capital budgeting problem.

criteria.name: Assumption Articulation
criteria.description: Ability to articulate clear and relevant assumptions underlying the capital budgeting analysis.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Provides well-reasoned assumptions that align with financial principles and the specifics of the problem.
criteria.poor_indicator: Assumptions are vague, irrelevant, or contradict basic financial principles.

criteria.name: Task Definition
criteria.description: Ability to translate the ill-defined problem into a well-defined task with clear objectives and constraints.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Formulates a specific, actionable task that is logically structured and considers all given constraints.
criteria.poor_indicator: The task is vague, lacks clear objectives, or ignores key constraints.

typical_errors: Overlooking the impact of discount rates on cash flow valuation.
Misinterpreting the time horizon for the investment.
Ignoring inflation effects on future cash flows.

criteria.name: Error Identification
criteria.description: Ability to identify errors or gaps in the AI's analysis of capital budgeting scenarios.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Accurately pinpoints specific errors or omissions in cash flow projections, discount rate applications, or NPV calculations.
criteria.poor_indicator: Fails to notice significant errors or gaps, or incorrectly identifies non-existent issues.

criteria.name: Financial Principle Application
criteria.description: Ability to apply relevant financial principles to judge the AI's output.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Uses sound financial reasoning to assess the accuracy and relevance of the AI's output.
criteria.poor_indicator: Relies on superficial or incorrect financial principles, leading to flawed judgments.

criteria.name: Result Consistency
criteria.description: Ability to evaluate the consistency of AI output with the initial problem framing and assumptions.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Ensures that AI outputs align with the initial problem framing and assumptions.
criteria.poor_indicator: Overlooks inconsistencies between AI output and the initial framing or assumptions.

typical_errors: Misjudging the impact of tax adjustments on cash flows.
Overlooking inconsistencies in the AI's projected cash flows and discount rates.
Failing to recognize incorrect application of the payback period.

criteria.name: Correction Command Clarity
criteria.description: Ability to provide clear and precise correction commands to the AI based on identified errors.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Commands are specific, actionable, and address the identified errors directly.
criteria.poor_indicator: Commands are vague, general, or fail to address the identified errors effectively.

criteria.name: Feedback Integration
criteria.description: Ability to integrate new information or feedback into the AI's analysis to improve accuracy.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Successfully incorporates new data or feedback to refine the AI's analysis and output.
criteria.poor_indicator: Fails to integrate new information effectively, resulting in minimal improvement.

criteria.name: Iterative Improvement
criteria.description: Ability to iteratively refine AI output through multiple cycles of judgment and steering.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Consistently improves the AI's output through well-reasoned iterative cycles.
criteria.poor_indicator: Shows little to no improvement over multiple iterations, or introduces new errors.

typical_errors: Providing overly generic correction commands that do not address specific errors.
Failing to update assumptions or inputs based on new information.
Neglecting to check if previous corrections have been implemented correctly.
